payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the active, non-superseded memory attached to Sam that references Goldman. mem_old was excluded because it is explicitly superseded, so showing it as a current result would risk presenting outdated information. The recall path prefers the latest valid memory in a conflicting fact cluster and filters older superseded records from normal results.

entity_resolution.reason: Recall matched the query entity to the existing person record for Sam; the returned memory was then selected from that entity's active, non-superseded memories.

memory_cards.statement: Sam is associated with Goldman in the current memory state via mem_new.
mem_old was filtered because it is marked superseded by a newer memory for the same subject/fact cluster.

details: Resolved query target to entity Sam.
Found active memory mem_new referencing Goldman.
Detected mem_old as superseded by mem_new.
Applied recall policy to suppress superseded memories from current results.

repair_options: Inspect the supersedes edge between mem_new and mem_old.
Verify both memories are linked to the same Sam entity and fact cluster.
Check whether mem_old should remain hidden in standard recall but visible in audit/history mode.
If filtering seems wrong, review timestamps, conflict-resolution rules, and deletion/supersession metadata.
